요한복음 2:1.content: The third day, there was a wedding in Cana of Galilee. Jesus’ mother was there.

요한복음 2:2.content: Jesus also was invited, with his disciples, to the wedding.

요한복음 2:3.content: When the wine ran out, Jesus’ mother said to him, “They have no wine.”

요한복음 2:4.content: Jesus said to her, “Woman, what does that have to do with you and me? My hour has not yet come.”

요한복음 2:5.content: His mother said to the servants, “Whatever he says to you, do it.”

요한복음 2:6.content: Now there were six water pots of stone set there after the Jews’ way of purifying, containing two or three metretes apiece.

요한복음 2:7.content: Jesus said to them, “Fill the water pots with water.” So they filled them up to the brim.

요한복음 2:8.content: He said to them, “Now draw some out, and take it to the ruler of the feast.” So they took it.

요한복음 2:9.content: When the ruler of the feast tasted the water now become wine, and didn’t know where it came from (but the servants who had drawn the water knew), the ruler of the feast called the bridegroom

요한복음 2:10.content: and said to him, “Everyone serves the good wine first, and when the guests have drunk freely, then that which is worse. You have kept the good wine until now!”

요한복음 2:11.content: This beginning of his signs Jesus did in Cana of Galilee, and revealed his glory; and his disciples believed in him.

요한복음 2:12.content: After this, he went down to Capernaum, he, and his mother, his brothers, and his disciples; and they stayed there a few days.

요한복음 2:13.content: The Passover of the Jews was at hand, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.

요한복음 2:14.content: He found in the temple those who sold oxen, sheep, and doves, and the changers of money sitting.

요한복음 2:15.content: He made a whip of cords, and threw all out of the temple, both the sheep and the oxen; and he poured out the changers’ money and overthrew their tables.

요한복음 2:16.content: To those who sold the doves, he said, “Take these things out of here! Don’t make my Father’s house a marketplace!”

요한복음 2:17.content: His disciples remembered that it was written, “Zeal for your house will eat me up.”

요한복음 2:18.content: The Jews therefore answered him, “What sign do you show us, seeing that you do these things?”

요한복음 2:19.content: Jesus answered them, “Destroy this temple, and in three days I will raise it up.”

요한복음 2:20.content: The Jews therefore said, “It took forty-six years to build this temple! Will you raise it up in three days?”

요한복음 2:21.content: But he spoke of the temple of his body.

요한복음 2:22.content: When therefore he was raised from the dead, his disciples remembered that he said this, and they believed the Scripture, and the word which Jesus had said.

요한복음 2:23.content: Now when he was in Jerusalem at the Passover, during the feast, many believed in his name, observing his signs which he did.

요한복음 2:24.content: But Jesus didn’t entrust himself to them, because he knew everyone,

요한복음 2:25.content: and because he didn’t need for anyone to testify concerning man; for he himself knew what was in man.
